brief_document.title: Generated wire types and typed REST client
brief_document.purpose: Give the dashboard a single, typed door to the server's REST surface and ensure its wire types are generated from the engine's Rust types so the UI cannot drift out of sync with the API contract.
brief_document.task: Source the wire types (Event, WorkflowSummary, WorkflowStatus, WorkflowFilter, namespace) from the house generated-types pipeline into src/types/generated, and build a typed REST client over the server's list/query, history, and namespaces endpoints. Isolate every not-yet-pinned AW contract detail in the client so a contract change is a one-file edit. Keep local-only view models separate from generated wire types.

requirements.title: Generated wire types
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL source the Aion wire types from the house generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] on aion-core/aion-proto types → src/types/generated). THE SYSTEM SHALL NOT hand-define a type the engine owns, and SHALL NOT hand-edit generated files.
acceptance: src/types/generated holds the generated TypeScript for Event (all variants), WorkflowSummary, WorkflowStatus, WorkflowFilter, and the namespace type.
The generation is wired through the same mechanism apps/web uses (the just gen-types / ts-rs path), documented in the app README.
src/types/index.ts re-exports the generated wire types and holds no hand-written copy of a generated shape.
No generated file is hand-edited (a comment header marking it generated is present).

requirements.title: Typed REST client over the AW surface
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a typed REST client exposing listWorkflows/queryWorkflows (taking a WorkflowFilter plus pagination), getHistory(workflowId) returning the ordered Event list, and listNamespaces, each returning typed results and typed errors.
acceptance: src/lib/api/client.ts exposes queryWorkflows(filter, page) → typed page of WorkflowSummary, getHistory(workflowId) → typed Event[], and listNamespaces() → typed namespace list.
Requests carry the namespace and whatever credential the server expects (passed in, not invented here).
Failures surface as a typed ApiError carrying status and message, mirroring apps/web's api/base pattern.
src/lib/api/index.ts barrel re-exports the client and its types.
A unit test with a mocked fetch asserts each method parses a typed result and maps a failure to ApiError.

requirements.title: Contract isolation for not-yet-pinned AW details
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL isolate every AW contract detail that is not yet finalised (endpoint paths, query/pagination parameter names, response envelope shape) in the client module so that a contract change is a single-file edit.
acceptance: All endpoint path strings and parameter names live as named constants/config in client.ts, not scattered across feature code.
The pagination contract (page/cursor) is expressed once in the client and consumed via the client's typed return, so features never construct raw query strings.
An inline comment documents that these are the AW contract surface and the single place to update when AW pins the contract.
No feature module constructs a raw URL or query string against the server.

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the WebSocket manager — that is AU-003.
SHALL NOT define server endpoints or the wire protocol — that is cluster AW; this brief codes against it.
SHALL NOT build any view — AU-004, AU-005.
verification: bun test passes for the client unit tests.
No generated file hand-edited; no lint-disable comments added.

checklist.text: Wire types (Event, WorkflowSummary, WorkflowStatus, WorkflowFilter, namespace) are sourced from the generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] → src/types/generated/), not hand-defined, and generated files are not hand-edited.
A typed REST client exposes listWorkflows/queryWorkflows (filter + pagination), getHistory(workflowId), and listNamespaces, each returning typed results.
The REST client returns typed errors and the not-yet-pinned AW contract details (paths, parameter names, pagination shape) are isolated in this one module.
Local-only view models (timeline entry, filter form state) are hand-written in feature types.ts and kept distinct from generated wire types.

stories.text: As a frontend maintainer, I want wire types generated from the Rust engine types so that the dashboard cannot silently drift out of sync with the server's API contract.
As a frontend maintainer, I want the server contract isolated in one api module so that when the server's endpoint or protocol detail is finalised I change it in one place.

constraints.text: Match the apps/web stack exactly: React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind v4, Bun, Biome (100-char), shadcn/Radix primitives, TanStack Query. No new framework, styling system, or state library.

Wire types come from the house generated-types pipeline (Rust #[derive(TS)] → src/types/generated/). The dashboard never hand-defines a type aion-core/aion-proto owns and never hand-edits a generated file.

The dashboard consumes the AW API/WebSocket contract; it defines no server endpoint, no WebSocket protocol, and no asset-hosting mechanism. Not-yet-pinned contract details are isolated in the api module for a one-file change.
Feature-folder convention from apps/web: each feature is components/ + hooks/ (+ context/lib as needed) + types.ts + index.ts. Barrels re-export; logic lives in named files.
Server state goes through TanStack Query; live updates flow through the WebSocket manager and patch query caches or local view state. No bespoke fetch-into-useState for server data.
Every async surface renders explicit loading, empty, and error states. No surface spins indefinitely or blanks on failure.

The WebSocket manager reconnects with bounded backoff on drop and the active view resyncs (re-fetch history/query, or replay after last-seen sequence where supported). A connection indicator is always present; a dropped socket never leaves a silently stale view.
The event timeline is ordered strictly by event sequence number and de-duplicates events arriving via both history fetch and live stream.
Biome lint + format clean (bun run check) and tsc --noEmit clean. No lint-disable comments to silence findings.
Namespace selection scopes every query and every subscription; switching namespace re-scopes both. No unscoped query when a namespace is selected.

resolved_context.intention: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event history rendered as a timeline, and watch events stream live as they happen. This cluster builds the frontend only. It consumes the HTTP/gRPC list/query/history API and the WebSocket event stream that the server (cluster AW) exposes and hosts as static assets; it does not run the engine, persist anything, or define the API. It must match the existing house frontend stack (React 19 + TypeScript + Vite + Tailwind v4 + Biome + Bun, shadcn/Radix, TanStack Query, a singleton WebSocket manager with reconnect, generated types from Rust) exactly, so it reads as part of the same codebase as apps/web. When done, an operator pointed at a running aion-server can observe every workflow in every namespace, drill into any execution's full history, and watch events stream in real time — with production-grade loading, empty, error, and reconnection behaviour throughout.

provenance.quote: Build the human-facing operational web UI for aion-server: a browser application that lets an operator see which workflows are running and which have failed, drill into a single execution's full event
